metadata.archetype_justification: DVC is a locally-installed Python CLI tool and library for ML data version control. It has no persistent data store, no HTTP/RPC server surface, no authentication surface, and no write operations exposed via an API.
metadata.context: Data Version Control: git-for-ML-data, models, and experiments.
metadata.dev_library_application_rationale: Service archetype is stateless-utility AND 5/5 surface flags are false. Per Step 1.5, library N/A mapping applied.

findings.title: Infrastructure Governance — Not Applicable for CLI Library
findings.description: DVC is a locally-installed CLI tool distributed via PyPI. It does not own API gateways, IAM roles, or networking infrastructure. The library's engineering governance is its own build/release pipeline (GitHub Actions CI/CD).
findings.gap: Not applicable — DVC does not own integration infrastructure.
findings.recommendation: No action required for DVC. Consumers building agent tooling around DVC should define their own IaC governance.

findings.title: CI/CD with API Contract Testing — No API Surface
findings.description: No HTTP/RPC surface — API contract testing is not applicable. DVC has a well-structured CI/CD pipeline via GitHub Actions with comprehensive test coverage running across multiple platforms and Python versions. Library contract stability is evaluated by typed exports and semantic versioning.
findings.gap: Not applicable — no API surface to contract-test.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Existing CI/CD pipeline provides strong contract assurance for the Python library interface.

findings.title: Rollback Capability — Consumer Responsibility
findings.description: No deployed HTTP/RPC surface — deployment rollback is a consumer concern. DVC is distributed as a pip package via PyPI. Library rollback is handled via package version pinning by consumers.
findings.gap: Not applicable — no deployed surface to roll back.
findings.recommendation: No action required. PyPI versioning and consumer-side pinning provide adequate rollback for library dependencies.

findings.title: API Test Coverage — Comprehensive Library Testing
findings.description: DVC has a comprehensive test suite (~256 test files) with pytest across unit, functional, and integration tests. Coverage is reported via Codecov. Since DVC exposes no HTTP/RPC surface, API test coverage for agent-consumable endpoints is not applicable.
findings.gap: Not applicable — no API surface requiring agent-specific test coverage.
findings.recommendation: No action required for current scope. If DVC is wrapped as an agent tool, the wrapper layer should add its own test coverage.

findings.title: Encryption at Rest — Consumer-Managed Backends
findings.description: DVC does not own or manage persistent data stores. It manages references (.dvc files) to data stored in remote backends (S3, GCS, Azure). Encryption at rest for those backends is configured at the storage layer by the consumer, not by DVC itself.
findings.gap: Not applicable — DVC does not own data stores.
findings.recommendation: No action required for DVC. Document that consumers should enable encryption at rest on their configured DVC remotes.
